agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby capital project program review final report
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider the Stewart Group's final report on Burnaby's capital project program. The report recommends a formal design-to-budget policy requiring all projects over $25 million to operate within firm affordability limits, with mandatory reviews triggered when estimates exceed 10% of the original budget. It also recommends a dedicated strategy to help Burnaby-based small and medium enterprises secure City contracts.

agenda_briefs.article_title: Committee of the Whole Meeting Agenda — May 12, 2026

minutes_briefs.title: Council adopted 10 recommendations from Stewart Group capital project review
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously adopted all 10 recommendations from The Stewart Group's strategic review of the City's Capital Project Program. The recommendations are designed to strengthen how the city plans and delivers capital projects, ensuring better value for infrastructure investments. Key changes include a new Design to Build Policy to keep projects on budget, consistent project close-out reports, a strategy to help small and local contractors win city work, a framework to assess who benefits from projects and who bears the costs, and a plan to build in-house project management capacity. Council also approved ongoing workshops to improve its own oversight of capital planning.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you are a small or local contractor, prepare to register for the city's new Small and Local Contractor Strategy to access upcoming capital project opportunities.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
3.1
VERBAL REPORT: BURNABY CAPITAL PROJECT PROGRAM REVIEW (THE
STEWART GROUP)
Lecia Stewart, President and CEO of The Stewart Group, and Charlotte
Lethwaite, Director of Advisory Services, presented the final report on the
strategic review of the City of Burnaby’s Capital Project Program to Council.
L. Stewart explained that the primary objective of the review was to strengthen
capital planning and delivery processes to maximize the value of future
infrastructure investments. The review identified ten core, ready-to-implement
recommendations, organized into three phases:
Phase 1
•
Design to Build Policy: Emphasizes early-stage controls and formal stage
gates to ensure projects remain within approved budgets and to maintain
transparency when budget adjustments are required.
•
Project Close-Out Reports: Establishes consistent reporting at project
completion to capture outcomes, lessons learned, and financial performance.
•
Small and Local Contractor Strategy: Aims to improve access for local
suppliers, supporting the local economy and keeping capital investment within
the community.
Phase 2
•
Benefit-Burden Review: Introduces a framework to assess the distribution of
project benefits and associated costs.
•
Transparent Reporting Culture: Promotes open, consistent reporting practices
across capital projects.

Open Committee of the Whole Meeting
Minutes May 12, 2026
________________________________________________________________________________________

3
•
Council Capital Projects Workshop Series: Recommends ongoing workshops
to enhance Council’s understanding and oversight of capital planning and
delivery.
Phase 3
•
Project Deliverables and Accountability Framework: Defines clear
deliverables and accountability structures for project execution.
•
Project Authority Matrix: Clarifies decision-making authority and escalation
processes.
•
Incubator Stream for Innovative Pilots: Creates a mechanism to test and
scale innovative project approaches.
•
Own In-House Capital Project Management Function: Recommends building
internal capacity to improve efficiency, strengthen accountability, and support
organizational learning across projects.
Arising from discussion, the following motion was introduced:
MOVED AND SECONDED
THAT the 10 (ten) recommendations provided by The Stewart Group at the Open
Committee of the Whole meeting on May 12, 2026, be adopted.


CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY
